title: JavaScript heap out of memory error in workflows
symptom: Workflows crash with a 'JavaScript Heap Out of Memory' error when processing large datasets.
root_cause: The error occurs when the Node.js process runs out of memory while trying to handle large datasets. This can happen if the workflow processes too much data at once or if inefficient nodes are used, leading to high memory consumption.
fix_steps: Break the workflow into smaller chunks using the Split In Batches node to process 200 rows at a time.
Move logic out of the Code node and utilize built-in nodes like Filter or Aggregate to reduce memory usage.
Increase the execution timeout by setting the EXECUTIONS_PROCESS_TIMEOUT environment variable to 3600 seconds in the .env file.
Ensure the server has enough RAM if self-hosting.
agent_instruction: Implement batch processing and optimize node usage to manage memory effectively.
